post:
Just had an fascinating conversation with Paris-Roubaix legend George Hincapie about this Sunday's Queen of the Classics.

While most are expecting another Pogačar masterclass, Hincapie thinks Mathieu van der Poel has the advantage. The reason? Team depth. Alpecin's flat powerhouses like Philipsen can stay with Vanderpoel deeper into the race, while UAE's climber-heavy roster might leave Pogačar isolated when it matters.

What struck me most was Hincapie's insight about equipment evolution. We've gone from 25mm tires at 140psi to 32mm tubeless setups at 55-65psi. The result? Riders rarely walk sections anymore. The technology is so much more forgiving that what looks easier on TV is actually enabling harder racing.

The other key insight: position into cobble sectors isn't about watts - it's about crash avoidance. As Hincapie put it, "If there's a crash in 20th wheel, the whole peloton's blocked." Risk management trumps power output in those opening kilometers.

Full conversation: https://roadmancycling.com/podcast/ep-2536-hincape-opens-up-about-how-pogacar-can-win-roubaix
